Block.Para: An Act to amend the Employment Insurance Act (benefit period increase)

This bill was introduced during the First Session of the 39th Parliament. Pursuant to the Standing Orders of the House of Commons, it is deemed to have been considered and approved at all stages completed at the time of prorogation of the First Session. The number of the bill remains unchanged.

Also available on the Parliament of Canada Web Site at the following address:
http://www.parl.gc.ca
This enactment amends the Employment Insurance Act to increase the benefit period for claimants 45 years of age or over who are laid off permanently after 10 years or more in the labour force.

Bill_Part.RunningHead_E: Employment Insurance (single qualification period and benefit period increase)

Block.Para: An Act to amend the Employment Insurance Act (benefit period increase)
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:
1. Subsection 10(2) of the Employment Insurance Act is replaced by the following:
(2) The length of a benefit period is 52 weeks, except as otherwise provided in subsections (2.1), (10) to (15) and section 24.

Para.change: (2.1) The period of 52 weeks established by subsection (2) shall be increased, on one occasion only, by 2 weeks for every year that a claimant has been in the labour force, up to a maximum of 13 years, where the claimant
(a) becomes unemployed as a result of a permanent layoff by an employer;
(b) has been in the labour force for 10 years or more; and
(c) is 45 years of age or more.

Block.Para:
Published under authority of the Speaker of the House of Commons
